notes: Chapter argument: ch. 28 does not narrate history but builds the doctrinal centerpiece the preceding Millerite/1844 chapters were constructed to support — it takes the 2300-days/1844 date (established as a factual claim earlier in the book) as settled and pivots entirely to unpacking its theological meaning: Daniel 7's judgment-scene vision is read typologically through the Levitical day-of-atonement/sanctuary-cleansing ritual to argue that in 1844 Christ moved from the holy to the most holy place to begin a pre-Advent 'investigative judgment' of professed believers' names in the books of record. Nearly the whole chapter is a chain of scripture citations (Daniel, Revelation, Psalms, prophets, Gospels, Hebrews) marshaled to support this interpretive superstructure, so almost every claim is either an attributed_quote (checkable only for textual fidelity) or a theological/interpretive assertion about judgment, Satan-as-accuser, the book of life, and the atonement — unverifiable by pipeline rule 5. The only claims with an independently checkable historical kernel are the 2300-days-to-1844 chronology (GC-28-083) and the Genesis flood/creation-week references, both of which survived verification as 'supported' (accurately reporting the Millerite/Adventist reckoning and the biblical text respectively), not as historical or scientific fact-claims independently confirmed by the pipeline. Stage notes: pass-through from adversarial stage confirms 0 contradicted, 0 misquotation, 0 anachronistic, 0 disputed among 135 claims (55 supported — mostly common-knowledge-fast-pathed faithful scripture quotations plus GC-28-083's accurate report of the Adventist 1844 reckoning — and 80 unverifiable theological/interpretive claims per rule 5). No claim in this chapter carries a final effective verdict of contradicted/misquotation/anachronistic, and no disputed item was judged high-stakes enough to score optionally, so no impact objects are produced for this chapter; the delta's items map is intentionally empty. Narrative_arc for this chapter as a whole, were any claim scoreable, would be doctrinal (investigative-judgment doctrine as the theological centerpiece built directly on the 1844 chronology verified/defended in earlier chapters, esp. GC-22 and the Millerite-history chapters) rather than millerite itself, since the chapter's own argument is exposition of doctrine, not narration of the 1844 movement's history. GC-28-083 (2300 days/1844) is noted specifically as the one date-type claim bridging this doctrinal chapter to the historical Millerite chain — it stayed supported (medium confidence) because it accurately reports the reckoning rather than asserting the underlying 457-BC/day-year premises as historical fact, so it carries no confirmed discrepancy to score, but flags it as the load-bearing hinge for any future synthesis-stage discussion of whether the investigative-judgment doctrine's historical anchor (1844) itself survives scrutiny even though no error was confirmed here.
